name: Izakaya Nonbei
tagline: Intimate Tokyo-style izakaya tucked on a side street — the bartender knows your name by the second visit.
mustOrder: yakitori — grilled over charcoal, proper izakaya style
parking: Street parking on Olu St and Kapahulu side streets
talkStory: Run by a Japanese chef-owner on tiny Olu Street off Kapahulu, Nonbei (meaning 'drinker' in Japanese) is the intimate, refined counterpart to Honolulu's louder izakayas. The small counter-bar setting genuinely feels like stumbling into a side-street joint in Tokyo's Shinjuku. Charcoal-grilled yakitori, pristine sashimi, and a sake list curated by someone who cares. The crowd is mostly local Japanese-American regulars and expats who know the real thing. Dinner only, reservations strongly recommended.
